title: Job Finding Assistance * Homelessness Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Job Finding Assistance * Homelessness Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions African American Community Homelessness Issues Seasonal Workers Substance Use Disorder Issues Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outreach assistance with housing and job search help, identification assistance (ID and Social Security cards), replacement birth certificate help with application and fee, general mail delivery, credit report requests, and SNAP and OHP application assistance. Also helps to connect people to services for savings, spending, educational plans, and life skills. Mobile Navigators meet clients in public spaces that work best for the client. PO Box 1972 Grants Pass, OR 97528 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 295-5706 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE DPI GROUP EMPLOYMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary and full-time employment opportunities for individuals facing employment barriers. 4950 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Age 18 and older People with disabilities, veterans, those experiencing homelessness, and those with justice system involvement Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 281-1289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID ROGUE FOUNDATION MOBILE NAVIGATION PROGRAM ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION THE DPI GROUP EMPLOYMENT SERVICES
